Erik Wilde: The Hidden Common Ground in Business Agents and Agentic Coding

(2) Erik Wilde

  • Head of Enterprise Strategy at Jentic [https://jentic.com/]

Agents & Platforms

Outline (Agents & Platforms)

  1. Agents & Platforms [5]
  2. Disconnect #1: AI-First without API-First [5]
  3. Disconnect #2: No Agent-Aware Security [4]
  4. Conclusions [3]
Agents & Platforms Erik Wilde: The Hidden Common Ground in Business Agents and Agentic Coding

(4) Coding Agents and Business Agents

  • Coding Agents
    • They change how the business does things
    • Tool side speed-up
    • Getting existing work done faster
  • Business Agents
    • They change what the business does
    • Business-side reinvention
    • From narrow off-the-shelf to broad and ambitious


Agents & Platforms Erik Wilde: The Hidden Common Ground in Business Agents and Agentic Coding

(5) Differences and Commonalities

  • Different in obvious ways
    • Coding agents arrive via platform engineering
    • Business agents arrive via business strategy
  • Common in less obvious ways
    • 97% of developers use coding agents
    • Business agents keep arriving, at least as COTS
    • Both need to plug in and integrate


Agents & Platforms Erik Wilde: The Hidden Common Ground in Business Agents and Agentic Coding

(6) The Platform Principle

  • You don't need a platform to ship one thing
  • Platforms are for shipping many similar things at scale
    • Shared substrate beats many bespoke stacks
    • Capabilities, governance, security → built for reuse
  • That's where both kinds of agents are heading
    • Coding agents: already at scale; platform spirit emerging
    • Business agents: scale is the next problem to solve


Agents & Platforms Erik Wilde: The Hidden Common Ground in Business Agents and Agentic Coding

(7) The Optionality Principle

  • Fitness = the ability to experiment and learn fast
  • AI is where that pays off most right now
  • Disconnected stacks → fragmented optionality
  • Shared platform → unified optionality


Agents & Platforms Erik Wilde: The Hidden Common Ground in Business Agents and Agentic Coding

(8) What We Found

  • Many reports on AI scaling but none on this disconnect
  • We surveyed both sides
    • IT / Platform engineering
    • Business
    • 200+ responses across 50 organizations
  • Found 7 disconnects
  • Today: 2 of them
    • Disconnect #1: AI-First without API-First [Disconnect #1: AI-First without API-First (1)]
    • Disconnect #2: No Agent-Aware Security [Disconnect #2: No Agent-Aware Security (1)]


Disconnect #1: AI-First without API-First

Outline (Disconnect #1: AI-First without API-First)

  1. Agents & Platforms [5]
  2. Disconnect #1: AI-First without API-First [5]
  3. Disconnect #2: No Agent-Aware Security [4]
  4. Conclusions [3]
Disconnect #1: AI-First without API-First Erik Wilde: The Hidden Common Ground in Business Agents and Agentic Coding

(10) AI-First without API-First: The Problem

  • Agents getting connected to APIs (directly or via MCP)
  • API quality and AI-readiness not checked first
  • Broken or sub-optimal workflows now exposed to agents
  • Old problems carry into the new AI layer
  • API problems may surface as AI problems


Disconnect #1: AI-First without API-First Erik Wilde: The Hidden Common Ground in Business Agents and Agentic Coding

(11) AI-First without API-First: The Data



Disconnect #1: AI-First without API-First Erik Wilde: The Hidden Common Ground in Business Agents and Agentic Coding

(12) Instruktör: Agentic Confusion



Disconnect #1: AI-First without API-First Erik Wilde: The Hidden Common Ground in Business Agents and Agentic Coding

(13) AI-First without API-First: The Consequences

  • Platform foundations skipped under C-suite AI pressure
  • AI pilots: isolated results, scaling not part of the plan
  • Bad processes get scaled up, not fixed


Disconnect #1: AI-First without API-First Erik Wilde: The Hidden Common Ground in Business Agents and Agentic Coding

(14) Tactics: MCP-Washing is not a Strategy

  • Make AI-readiness a gate before agent integrations
  • Reuse and refactor existing API governance
  • Fix bad APIs & workflows before agents touch them
  • Don't paper over old problems with MCP


Disconnect #2: No Agent-Aware Security

Outline (Disconnect #2: No Agent-Aware Security)

  1. Agents & Platforms [5]
  2. Disconnect #1: AI-First without API-First [5]
  3. Disconnect #2: No Agent-Aware Security [4]
  4. Conclusions [3]
Disconnect #2: No Agent-Aware Security Erik Wilde: The Hidden Common Ground in Business Agents and Agentic Coding

(16) No Agent-Aware Security: The Problem

  • Agents are less predictable than traditional code
  • Coding agents produce code: reviewable before it runs
  • Business agents produce actions: live as they're decided
  • Both need limits but controls in place are mostly symbolic


Disconnect #2: No Agent-Aware Security Erik Wilde: The Hidden Common Ground in Business Agents and Agentic Coding

(17) No Agent-Aware Security: The Data



Disconnect #2: No Agent-Aware Security Erik Wilde: The Hidden Common Ground in Business Agents and Agentic Coding

(18) No Agent-Aware Security: The Consequences

  • Some controls exist, but no one is watching
  • Vulnerabilities and unusual patterns invisible
  • Context and action creep: agents access more than they need
  • Optionality [The Optionality Principle (1)] takes a hit when experiments feel unsafe


Disconnect #2: No Agent-Aware Security Erik Wilde: The Hidden Common Ground in Business Agents and Agentic Coding

(19) No Agent-Aware Security: Tactics

  • Add a control layer between agents and APIs
  • Default-deny: grant only what's needed
  • Monitor what agents do, not just that they have keys
  • Alerts + thresholds → human-in-the-loop


Conclusions

Outline (Conclusions)

  1. Agents & Platforms [5]
  2. Disconnect #1: AI-First without API-First [5]
  3. Disconnect #2: No Agent-Aware Security [4]
  4. Conclusions [3]
Conclusions Erik Wilde: The Hidden Common Ground in Business Agents and Agentic Coding

(21) AI Platform Tactics

  • One agent platform is the ideal, not the overnight plan
  • Treat both kinds of agents as one demand stream
  • Share governance, security, observability
  • Spot parallel platforms and manage them strategically


Conclusions Erik Wilde: The Hidden Common Ground in Business Agents and Agentic Coding

(22) More Disconnects in the Report
